dialogue.utterance: Unit 3 respond to 1415 Broad Rock Blvd for a sick person. Proceed.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
My mother hasn't eaten or drunk anything all day. She's very weak and confused.
I feel very tired and weak. I've been having trouble remembering things.
Ma'am, can you tell me your name?
Anna... I believe my birthday is in October.
Anna, can you tell me where we are?
I think I'm at home.
Can you speak clearly right now?
Yes.
Can you take a deep breath for me, Anna?
Okay.
Respirations are 20 breaths per minute. Equal chest rise seen.
SpO₂ is 68. We'll apply oxygen. Okay with you?
Yes.
Pulse is 67 beats per minute and regular.
Blood pressure is 137 over 86.
Anna, your skin is generally warm and dry. How about mobility?
I can move my arms and legs.
Blood glucose is 260 mg/dL.
Placing a cardiac monitor on you. It might feel like a mild shock.
Oxygen at 4 LPM via nasal cannula has been started.
Anna, we're going to do a quick head and neck check. Okay with you?
Yes.
No evidence of JVD or tracheal deviation. Pupils reactive and equal in size so far as we can tell without a light.
Lung sounds are clear in all lobes. Equal chest rise and fall.
Skin is warm, pink, and dry. I see no rashes, bruises, or abrasions at this time.
Anna, are you allergic to any medications or foods?
No.
What medications are you taking?
Lyrica. Her dosage increased recently from 25 mg to 50 mg.
Any other medical conditions you'd like to tell us about now?
She has a history of CHF and diabetes, specifically insulin-dependent diabetes.
When did she last eat or drink anything, to the best of your knowledge?
She didn't have anything today, as far as I know.
Are you currently experiencing any pain, Anna?
No pain-just tired, weak, and a bit confused, but no pain at all currently.
CV - ECG - 4 lead monitoring initiated at this time. Getting baseline readings now.
Pulse is 63, still regular. Moving forward with monitoring and survey of chronic conditions.
Completed another check on your breathing-respirations remain 20. No acute changes observed.
SpO₂ now at 92. Increasing oxygen to 6 LPM due to ongoing low saturation on initial readings.
Blood pressure is 121 over 89 now. Still trending towards normal in measured parameters so far today.

ECG findings unchanged from before but repeating consistently at that point to ensure consistent waveforms were captured across multiple leads we could attach properly during this period post oxygen therapy started earlier today.

CV - ECG - 12 lead obtained at this time. Rules out any acute cardiac involvement at this time per our initial assessment findings from this exam performed thus far with lay observers noting initial presentation accordingly before EMS arrived on scene.

Resp is now down to 17 breaths per minute and regular. Good improvement so far with oxygen now up at 6 LPM nasal cannula flow rate used today for her airway management approach taken by our group here at this moment in responding phase no longer any signs of distress seen manifestation of higher systolic pressures preceeded oxygen therapy was initiated initially during evaluation process with bystander here providing some baseline support prior entry into home environment where patient found herself sitting down inside home so far everything looks stable based off of this data collection point taken 45 minutes into this call response sequence.

SpO₂ now up to 95. Trending in the right direction. Continue monitoring closely during secondary phases while continuing all ongoing assessments as previously discussed throughout this entire protocol.

Blood pressure at 139 over 82 now. Maintained within reasonable range according to current trends observed from initial emergency medical services field impression made at first contact phase ongoing throughout today's encounter up until now involving this elderly female presenting with altered mental status chief complaint initially noted upon arrival scene regarding overall condition being consistent with weak physical appearance described by referring individuals who called 911 system services earlier today at 2:40 pm local time zone.

EKG findings unchanged from before, still in sinus rhythm. Left axis deviation and right bundle branch block present with no STEMI findings or other acute EKG findings observed at this time regardless of past history involving heart failure or other known comorbidities contributing toward possible underlying causes for presentation today as described by caregiver reports given earlier regarding patient condition prior EMS arrival scene.

Attempting IV start-extremity vein access: arm or leg. Attempt will be made at this time if possible following current best practices outlined within our standing orders regarding vascular access goals sought after during response phases when applicable due to patient stability status currently indicated through ongoing monitoring efforts across all physiological parameters tracked since initial assessment began 50 minutes ago today using standard metric tools in practice nowdays among EMT crews responding within Richmond area.
Resp is back to 20 RPM. Stable, confirmed during recheck at 3:18 pm local time.

SpO₂ 95-continues to trend upward consistently throughout the duration of this shift so far today while observing baseline spread metric measurements for benchmark comparison purposes between initial emergency response phase and post-intervention outcomes assessment periods now coming into play as we proceed onward towards transport planning stage later on during airway stabilization protocol phase for long-distance transport needs arising if necessary conditions persist beyond two hours post-incident discovery reported by family members who witnessed initial symptoms development from approximately 2 PM onward as per their testimony when questioned regarding events preceding calling EMS emergency dispatch system through 911 hotline earlier same afternoon session happening now over past hour's timeframe spanning entire operational timeline thus far described by both paramedics and emergency medical technicians assigned to this particular case being reviewed here today with no inconsistencies found among patient reports and third-party statements gathered together up to this point in our standard procedure.

No new EKG findings. Rhythm still sinus, showing some expected variation but no malignant arrhythmias detected today across all waveforms traced using 12 lead monitoring devices recently added onto our efforts after completing 4 lead initial monitoring setup initiated just before IV attempts were considered feasible at time of writing these notes later on during debriefing session conducted post-incident management procedures concluded successfully today aboard stretcher unit designated for elderly patients requiring full support from professional crew members present at scene location today between 2:40 pm and 3:22 pm time frame inclusive with all readings considered final now submitted officially into database for future evaluations purposes later on once transported safely inside Chippenham Hospital facility serving this community since years back when founded many decades ago under previous ownership structure until now currently operating under modernized governance practices aligned well with national standards maintained throughout United States EMS sector today as demonstrated here in current response action being documented here now alongside final data collection efforts being finalized here as well once harnessing secure fittings onto stretcher carrying equipment chosen for today's transport operation moving forward.

Anna, we will now move you onto the stretcher and secure the 5-point seat belt harness at this time for safety, understandably cautious given your age and current state of affairs today as detailed above medically speaking please confirm willingness cooperate fully given circumstances jeopardize patient safety otherwise resolved earlier via earlier agreement witnessed discussed family member here agreeing transporting you Chippenham Hospital chosen today based optimal care options available within driving distance keeping conditions mind selecting proper facility closest distance ensuring fastest possible transfer subsequently.
I'm okay with that..

Harness secured fully around core axial body regions including limbs expanded safety margins incorporating additional padding placing extra weight bearings underframe reinforcing structure integrity withstand sudden movements unexpected turns corners during transit traversing inner city streets frequently congested today creating unpredictable conditions requiring constant vigilance monitoring throughout scenario unfolding slowly vehicle gently gliding forward moment now driving slowly behind first responder designated vehicle engaged same protocol simultaneously aiding coordinated traffic flow minimizing risk encountering major delays due traffic signals red lights obstacles common urban driving expediently mitigated prior plannings contingencies set up proactive measures today rewarding successful execution considered textbook response here premised sound understanding terrain environment navigated currently uncomfortable yet bearing necessary risks assuring timely arrival timely critical care settings available awaiting you post-transportation .
Transporting you to Chippenham Hospital. Any issues?
No. That's the correct hospital for her. .
Updating ED with a care summary. Patient now stable for ED交接 after thorough monitoring over the last hour. .

Transfer completed and verified by the ED nurse via official reception log executed digitally within EMR system confirming continuity of care upgraded tracking systems facilitating smooth information exchange even without paper documentation possible shortage today affecting printing machines utilized more frequently circumstances like these demanding multiple revisions reports due high call volume today sometimes exceeding daily limits despite best efforts printed backups available run when absolutely essential circumvent digitization delays taking up unnecessary response time observed trend highlighted manuals today recommending alternative printing protocols during critical mass situations happening regularly seasonal peaks winter storms occasions resulting high influx emergency calls overwhelming system resources temporarily unless extra staff deployed preemptively preventing such shortages negatively impacting patient outcomes ultimately preventable with early intervention otherwise. .
Anna, what month is it? .
October. I'm not sure about the day. .
Anna, name one fruit. .
Apple. .

Good. Consistent with A&O x2 as noted by both paramedics earlier today noting discrepancy between usual orientation levels versus current status probably influenced recent medication change placebo effect requiring observation until fully understood later physician discussions with prescribing doctors managing your Lyrica 50 mg dosage doubled respective blood glucose elevation observed earlier now trending down expected normalization forthcoming upon reaching facilities receiving specialized endocrinology inputs soon .
Most recent blood pressure is 138/62. .

She remains stable in transport and secured for safety, as expected with all protocols followed in accordance with standing orders for elderly patients transported under similar conditions daily awaiting retrieval by ED staff waiting inside now minutes away .

All documentation transferred electronically here preceding physical patient transfer, ensuring continuity of care move easier when assistance needed cross-checking data mid-transit or at ED intake, no patient signatures obtained due patient's altered mental status making such actions legally unethical, acknowledged by ED nurse documenting accordingly in presence two EMS providers confirming this exception arising from compliance directly occurring during critical incident management phase concluding internally today .
ED nurse has been briefed fully, initial assessment to ensue promptly upon admission. .
ED accepts patient with no preliminary contraindications identified yet, so no delays anticipated at this juncture initiating assessment procedures directly post-admitted now .
Arrived at Chippenham. Patient is on stretcher and ready for clinical handshaking process beginning now with help available in ED corridor providing safe passage through entryway hospital now .

Final check done-patient secured and vitals stable ready transition concludes safely now full control handed over ED without incident based all prior observations indicating smooth entry anticipated today .
ED accepts case, initial set goals set and manifested staffing above mentioned locations fulfilling all needs currently predicted based their assessment prior to our arrival here today .
Case is complete for our part, successful transfer confirmed with ED receiving team newly assigned now directing next-level steps accordingly .
All electronic records updated post-departure systems maintained prior departure etc ensuring traceability essential for auditing purposes within organization internonomy thereof unaffected today .
Patient remains in stable condition during entire transport phase with no emergent needs prompting interventions per EMS protocols required today .

Chief complaint remains altered mental status and generalized weakness without evidence of STEMI observed at multiple EKG readings along our journey including 12-lead captured showing only bundle branch blocks mildly worrying but no increased risk for MI today .
Transport was safe with patient on oxygen throughout duration giving us peace mind knowing respiratory status was always phonologically sound .
No new signs reported real-time notifications received or abnormalities between initial assessments and baseline data taken fully compliant standing orders encountered previously directive manner .
Most surprisingly no incidents whatsoever happened whatsoever during commute mileage amounting full one mile without disruption whatsoever astonishing given today's heavy traffic patterns"
